Container.Name: taskchampion-sync-server
Container.Overview: TaskChampion Sync Server is the official sync backend for Taskwarrior 3.x. It implements the TaskChampion sync protocol, allowing tasks to be synchronised across multiple devices. Task data is encrypted client-side; the server never sees plaintext. Note: the upstream taskchampion-sync-server provides no HTTP authentication by design — access control is via UUID allowlisting only. For internet-facing deployments, place this behind a reverse proxy with an auth layer (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager with HTTP Basic Auth).
Container.Requires: Clients must use Taskwarrior 3.0 or any app implementing the TaskChampion sync protocol. Each client needs a unique UUID (generate with `uuidgen`) and a shared encryption secret.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port mapped to the container's internal port 8080. Default is 8007 to avoid conflicts with common Unraid services.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite task database.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: UID the server process runs as. Defaults to Unraid's nobody user (99). Change to match your user ID on other systems (run `id` to find it).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: GID the server process runs as. Defaults to Unraid's users group (100). Change to match your group ID on other systems (run `id` to find it).

Config.Name: Client ID(s)
Config.Description: UUID(s) allowed to sync. Comma-separated for multiple devices (e.g. uuid1,uuid2). If left empty, any client can sync — set this if the server is reachable from outside your LAN. Generate a UUID with `uuidgen`.

Config.Name: No Create Clients
Config.Description: Set to true to prevent new clients from registering after initial setup. Only useful after all your devices have synced at least once — enabling this before first sync will block even allowed clients.

Config.Name: Listen Address
Config.Description: Address and port the server binds to inside the container. Only change this if you also adjust the container port above.
